summary: Hypotension begins with moderate-to-severe blood loss of about one-third to half total circulating blood volume; this corresponds clinically to signs such as decreased perfusion evident by class III or IV hemodynamic states described in classification systems for hemorrhage severity. Agent1 reasoned based on typical thresholds from medical literature linking specific percentages of blood loss directly to observable physiological responses including hypotensive episodes during acute bleeding scenarios. The summary concisely captures these critical aspects related to recognizing early warning signs through quantifiable benchmarks tied closely to patient outcomes under varying degrees of trauma-induced anemia leading up to life-threatening conditions necessitating prompt intervention measures aimed primarily towards restoring intravascular volumes promptly via fluid resuscitation
